title: ranunculus abortivus var. typicus
intro: Ranunculus abortivus var. typicus, also known as the small-flowered buttercup or small-flowered crowfoot, is a perennial plant that belongs to the Ranunculaceae family. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to have health benefits such as soothing digestive issues, reducing fever, and treating skin irritations, although more research is needed to confirm these uses. Herbal preparations of Ranunculus abortivus var. typicus often include infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be used externally or internally to harness its medicinal properties.
